English: What are some of the climatic conditions that favor avocado growing?
Kikuyu: Nī rīera rīrīkū rīteithagīrīria gūkūra wega kwa mītī ya mīkorobia?

English: Avocado performs best between 1500-2100m a.s.l. with 1000 mm of well-distributed rainfall
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia īkūraga wega gatagatī-inī ka mita 1500-2100 igūrū kūma iriainī, na 100mm cia mbura njega.

English: It has very low tolerance to salinity.
Kikuyu: Mūceni na thumu nīwagīrīire kuongererwo harī tīīri ūtarī mūnoru

English: If the pH is above 6.5, Gypsum (CaSO4) may be used to lower it.
Kikuyu: Angīkorwo ūrūrū wa tīīri nī makīria ma 6.5, CaSO4 noītūmīke kūmīgūithia.

English: 1200 to 2200 meters above sea level
Kikuyu: Ūraihu wa mita 1200-2200 kūuma iria-inī.

English: Why does Kenya present a  favourable climate  to avocado production?
Kikuyu: Nīkīī gītūmaga būrūri wa Kenya wonekane wīna rīera rīega rīa kūrīma makorobia?

English: Avocado trees require tropical or subtropical climate because they can’t tolerate either freezing or extreme heat.
Kikuyu: Mītī ya mīkorobia īkūraga wega kūndū kwīna rīera ihehu na kūria kūrī gatagatī-inī nīundū ndīngītiria ūhehu mūkīru kana rīera ihiū mūno.

English: Kenya’s climate is perfectly suited for Hass avocado farming because it lies at the equator.
Kikuyu: Rīera rīa būrūri wa Kenya nīrīega na kūrīma mītī ya makorobia ya hass nīundū wīna rīera rīa gatagatī-inī

English: Hass avocados thrive in Kenya because different areas experience either equatorial or tropical climates.
Kikuyu: Mītī ya mīkorobia ya hass īkaga wega nīūndū icigo ngūrani ikoragwo na rīera rīa gatagatī-inī kana ihehu.

English: What are the ecological requirements for hass production?
Kikuyu: Nī rīera rīrīkū rīagīrīire ūrīmi wa mīkorobia ya hass?

English: Altitude range between 1000-2000m;
Kikuyu: Ūraihu wa kuma iria-inī wa gatagatī ka 1000-2000m.

English: Well distributed rainfall throughout the year of 1000mm-1600mm;
Kikuyu: Mūigana wa mbura wa 1000-1600mm īraura mwaka wī wothe.

English: Irrigation in dry season or in areas with inadequate rainfall;
Kikuyu: Kūhe maaī nī kwa bata mahīnda ma riūa inene kana icigo iria ciīna mbura ītarī njiganu.

English: Optimum temperatures of 20°C-24°C;
Kikuyu: Ūrugarī ūrīa wagīrīire nī wa gatagatī ka 20°-24°c.

English: Loose, sandy or loamy soils, well-draining with pH ranging 5-7;
Kikuyu: tīīri ūtarī mūnyitu ta wa rīūmba, wīna mūthanga,  ūtaraiga maaī na wīna ūrūrū wa gatagatī ka 5-7.

English: Minimum of 6 full hours of sunlight a day
Kikuyu: Mathaa matanyihīire 6 ma riūa mūthenya.

English: How is fertilizer top dressed in avocado?
Kikuyu: Mītī ya mīkorobia īheagwo bataraitha atīa?

English: What is the recommended amount of fertilizer that should be used in avocado growing?
Kikuyu: Nī mūigana ūrīkū wa bataraitha wītīkīrīkīte kūhūthīka harī ūrīmi wa mīkorobia?

English: Quantity of manure and fertiliser application is dependent on the soil fertility and the age of trees.
Kikuyu: Mūigana wa thumu na bataraitha ūringanaga na ūnoru wa tīīri na ūkūrū wa mītī.

English: Nitrogen is the most important nutrient in avocado.
Kikuyu: Nitrogen nīcio irio cia bata harī mīkorobia.

English: Basal fertilizers are absorbed by the plants through the roots and include DAP, CAN, NPK, UREA, among others.
Kikuyu: Bataraitha cia mūthingi itūmīkaga nī mīmera kūhītūkīra mīri nanītacio DAP, CAN, NPK, UREA na ingī nyingī.

English: Farmyard manure could also be added, depending on the organic matter of the soil
Kikuyu: Thumu wa mahiū nowongererwo kūringana na ūnoru wa tīīri.

English: What are some of the foliar fertilizers used in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Nī bataraitha irīkū cia mathangū ihūthīkaga harī ūrīmi wa makorobia?

English: They include OPTIMIZER, DIMIPHITE, ZINC GOLD, LAVENDER, GOLDCHANCE SERIES, VITABOR GOLD, among others.
Kikuyu: Nacio nīta:OPTIMIZER, DIMIPHITE, ZINC GOLD, LAVENDER, GOLDCHANCE SERIES, VITABOR GOLD na ingī nyingī.

English: How much water does it take to produce 1kg avocado?
Kikuyu: Nī mūigana ūrīku wa bataraitha ūngīthīkaga gūciarithia kiro īmwe ya makorobia?

English: You need 2,000 litres to produce a kg of avocado fruit
Kikuyu: Ūbataraga lita ngiri igīrī cia maaī gūciarithia kiro īmwe ya makorobia.

English: How is pollination enhanced in avocado orchard?
Kikuyu: Hihi gūciara kwa mītī ya makorobia kūngīgacīrithio atia?

English: The type of avocado cultivars planted in an orchard will contribute to the expected yield.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba ya mītī irīa mīhande nīyo īgūteithia kūgīa na maciaro marīa merīgīrīirūo.

English: There are two types of avocado that flower at different times of the year (type A and type B).
Kikuyu: Nī kūrī mīthemba īrī ya mītī ya avocado īrīa īcanūkana hīndī ngūrani mwaka-inī(Mūthemba A na B).

English: Hence cross pollination leads to higher yields than self -pollination.
Kikuyu: Nī ūndū ūcio, gūciarithania kwa mītī ngūrani nīkūrehaga maciaro maingī kūrī gūciarithania mītī ya mūthemba ūmwe.

English: The presence of bee hives within the orchard is recommended as bees are the main pollinators of avocado.
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūiga mīatū ya njūkī thīinī wa mīgūnda ya makorobia nīūndū njūkī nīcio irutaga wīra ūrīa mūingī wa gūciarithania mītī ya mīthemba ngūrani ya makorobia

English: One should avoid applying chemicals that are harmful to bees.
Kikuyu: Mūndū agīrīirūo gwīthema kūhūthīra dawa iria ingīūraga njūkī.

English: Avocado starts bearing within 3 years after transplanting.
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia yambagia gūciara mwaka-inī wa gatatū thutha wa kūmīruta tuta-inī.

English: Peak harvest occurs between July-August.
Kikuyu: Magetha maingīhaga gītagatī-inī kia mweri wa mūgwanja na wakanana.

English: Why is mulching important in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Mūūceni nī wa bata ūrīkū ūkūria-inī wa mīkorobia.

English: Mulching is undertaken to conserve moisture and to add organic matter to the soil.
Kikuyu: Mūūceni nī wa bata harī kūhonokia maaī na kūnoria tirī.

English: Sawdust should be avoided as it decomposes and ties up nitrogen.
Kikuyu: Mura ndwagīrīire kūhūthīka nīūndū nīwohaga nitrogen warīkia kūbutha.

English: Dry leaves may also be used as mulch.
Kikuyu: Mahuti momū no mahūthīrwo gūthondeka mūūceni.

English: Cultivate around the trees to keep them weed free.
Kikuyu: Rīma ūthiūrūrūkīirie mītī ūkīmunyaga ria rīothe.

English: Note that use of herbicides is not recommended.
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūmenya atī ūhūthīri wa dawa cia kūrīmīra ndwendekithītio.

English: How is pruning done in avocado?
Kikuyu: Mītī ya mīkorobia īcehagwo atīa?

English: Apical bud of young plants should be nipped to slow growth and lead to a compact tree.
Kikuyu: Mūrūri ūrīa wī harīa igūrū wagīrīire kūrengwo nīgetha mūtī ūtige kūraiha na waarame.

English: Lower branches that interfere with cultural activities including irrigation should be pruned.
Kikuyu: Honge cia thī iria iragiria mītī īrīmīkīre wega kana īitīīrīrio maaī na njīra njega ciagīrīire nī gūcehwo.

English: Which crop should be used for intercropping in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Nī mīmera īrīkū īngī īngīrīmwo hamwe na mīkorobia?

English: Avocado orchards may be intercropped with other crops such as beans, peas, kale, or cabbage during the first 3-5 years to get economic returns from the land before the trees start bearing or produce economic returns
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda wa mīkorobia noūhandwo mīmera īngī ta mboco, njūgū, matharū kana kabici mīaka ītatū kana īna ya mbere nīgetha ūingīhie umithio wa mūgūnda kamūiria magetha ma mīkorobia monekete.

English: What is the average yield per avocado tree?
Kikuyu: Mūigana wa magetha ma mīkorobia kaingī nīūrīkū?

English: An avocado tree yields 230-320 kg (7.5-11 t ha–1) of fruit per year.
Kikuyu: Mūtī wa mūkorobia ūciaraga gatagatī ga kiro 230-320kg kana tani 7.5-11 harī eka īmwe.

English: Grafted trees start bearing after 3-4 years after planting but economical crop is obtainable from the sixth year.
Kikuyu: Mūtī wa gūciarithanio wambagia gūciara thutha wa mīaka ītatū nginya īna no magetha maingī monekaga thutha wa mīaka ītandatū.

English: Which standard regulate avocado production in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī mūtaratara ūrīkū ūrūgamagīrīra ūrīmi wa makorobia gūkū Kenya?

English: Where can I get propagation materials for avocado be got?
Kikuyu: Hihi ingīruta kū indo cia gūciarithania mīkorobia?

English: Farmers can also plant seeds (local varieties) at farm and later graft using scions from registered mother blocks.
Kikuyu: Arīmi nomahande mbeū cia mītī ya kīmerera macoke mamīciarithanie na zayoni cia mītī īrīa mīhetūkie

English: The farmer can top-work the already existing avocado trees to introduce recommended varieties.
Kikuyu: Mūrīmi no athondeke mītī ya mīkorobia īrīa īrī kuo na njīra ya kūmīciarithania na mīthemba īrīa yagīrīire.

English: What are essential elements in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Hihi maūndū ma bata ūkūria-inī wa mīkorobia nī marīkū?

English: Potassium – important for yield and fruit quality.
Kikuyu: Potassium-nīyabata kūingīhia magetha na gūthakaria matunda.

English: What are the potassium deficiency symptoms in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Hihi nī maūndū marīkū monanagia ūnyihu wa potassium mītī-inī ya mīkorobia?

English: It is characterized by necrosis of leaf margin which starts from the top downwards.
Kikuyu: Wagi ūyū wonanagia kūbutha kwa mathangū mīthia-inī kuuma mwena wa naigūrū nginya wanathī.

English: Fruit quality is highly reduced.
Kikuyu: Ūthaka wa matunda ūkanyiha biū.

English: What are the boron deficiency symptoms in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Hihi ndariri cia ūnyihu wa boron nīirīkū?

English: It is characterized by apical and axillary growing points die off, leaves are distorted (crinkled with holes),
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia ūnyihu wa boron nī hamwe na gūkua gwa thuna, mathangū kūgūnyara na kūgīa na marima.

English: What are the advantages of grafting in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Hihi mawega ma gūceha mītī ya mīkorobia nīmarīkū?

English: Increases resistance to diseases;
Kikuyu: Nī kūongagīrīra hinya wa mītī kūrūa na mīrimū.

English: Increases  adaptability to different types of soils;
Kikuyu: Nī kūongagīrīra hinya wa mītī gūkūrīra tīīri-inī ngūrani.

English: Improve yield
Kikuyu: Nī kūongagīrīra maciaro

English: What are some of the field management practices in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Hihi njīra imwe cia gūtungata mītī ya mīkorobia nīirīkū?

English: Mulching;
Kikuyu: Gūthondeka mūūceni.

English: Supporting;
Kikuyu: Gūtira

English: Fertilizer Use;
Kikuyu: Kūhe bataraitha

English: Irrigation;
Kikuyu: Kūhe maaī

English: Pruning;
Kikuyu: Gūceha

English: Thinning;
Kikuyu: Gūthathaūra matunda mītī-inī

English: Weeding;
Kikuyu: Kūrīmīra

English: weed control;
Kikuyu: Kūhūrana na ria

English: pollination
Kikuyu: Gūciarithania

English: What is the importance of pruning in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Hihi bata wa gūceha mītī ya mīkorobia nīūrīkū?

English: Pruning is done at the early stages before flowering to remove premature flowers and fruits and after harvesting.
Kikuyu: Mītī īcehagwo tene ītacanūkīte nīguo kūruta mahūa na matunda maria matarendeka na ningī thutha wa kūgetha.

English: Why is thinning important in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Hihi bata wa gūthathaūra matunda mītī-inī ya mīkorobia nīūrīkū?

English: Why is pollination important in an avocado orchard and how can it be enhanced?
Kikuyu: Pollination nī ya bata ūrīkū ūrīmi-inī wa mīkorobia na īngīgacīrithio atīa?

English: Hass avocado trees are self-pollinating.
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia mūthemba wa Hass nīīciarithanagia.

English: But for a more plentiful harvest, farmers are advised to grow Hass with another variety like Fuerte for cross pollination.
Kikuyu: No nīguo magetha makorūo marī maingī, arīmi nī magīrīirio kūhanda Hass hamwe na mīthemba īngī ta Fuerte nīguo īgacenjania mbeū.

English: What is the nutritional content of avocado?
Kikuyu: Hihi ikorobia rīkoragwo na irio mūthemba ūrīkū?

English: The recommended rate of application is 4 tons per īka for Avocado
Kikuyu: Mūigana ūrīa wagīrīire gwīkīrwo nī tani inya harī īka īmwe ya mītī ya mīkorobia.

English: What is the most critical stage to ensure avocado trees are irrigated?
Kikuyu: Hihi ihinda rīrīa  rīa bata mūno rīa kūhe mītī ya mīkorobia maaī nī rīrīkū?

English: The most crucial period when additional irrigation water may be required is around mid January until April (the beginning of the long rains)
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīrīa rīkoragwo rīa bata mūnene gūitīīrīrīa maaī mangī nī kuuma gatagatī-inī ka Janūarī nginya April (kīambīrīria kīa mbura nene)

English: How would a farmer tell that avocados are ready for harvesting?
Kikuyu: Mūrīmī angīmenya atīa makorobia nī makūrū?

English: Since most varieties do not change colour on maturity, a few fruits should be picked and stored at room temperature for 7- 10 days.
Kikuyu: Nīūndū mīthemba mīingī ya makorobia maticenjagia rangi rīrīa makūra, makorobia manini magīrīire gūtūo na kūigwo nyūmba kwa ihinda rīa thikū mūgwanja nginya ikūmi.

English: At what stalk length should avocado be harvested?
Kikuyu: Ikorobia rīagīrīire gūtuo rīrī na kamūtī kaigana atia?

English: Fruit should not be pulled from the stalk but be cut off leaving a 3-cm stalk.
Kikuyu: Ikorobia rīagīrīire gūtuo kuuma mūtī-inī na rīrengwo hamwe na kamūtī kena ūraihu wa 3cm.

English: How should avocado be harvested?
Kikuyu: Makorobia magīrīire kūgethwo atia?

English: It is advisable to train harvesters on harvesting techniques, hygiene and proper handling as well as occupational health and safety.
Kikuyu: Nī wega gūthomithia atui a makorobia maūndū megiī njīra cia kūgetha, ūtheru na njīra njega ya kūmakuua, hamwe na maūndū megiī ūgima mwega wa mwīrī na ūgitīri rīrīa mararuta wīra.

English: Avocado fruits should be clipped from the trees with secateurs, leaving a portion of the stalk attached on the fruit (about 0.5cm long).
Kikuyu: Makorobia magīrīire gūtuo na magathī kuuma mītī-inī, na gūtigia kamūtī kanini kenyitīīrīire itunda-inī(ta 0.5cm).

English: Fruits should be harvested into soft bags or clean buckets and stored in clean crates.
Kikuyu: Makorobia mabatiī kūgetherwo mīhuko-inī mīororo kana ndoo-inī theru na macoke maigwo irīti-inī.

English: Harvesting records should be maintained by the farmer.
Kikuyu: Mūrīmi agīrīire kūiga rekondi cia magetha.

English: How does one determine maturity  in avocado?
Kikuyu: Mūndū amenyaga atīa ūkūrū wa makorobia?

English: Dry matter analysis.Dry matter is dependent on variety, region and season.
Kikuyu: Ūthuthuria wa dry matter. Dry matter īringanaga na mūthemba wa mūkorobia, itūra rīrīa ikorobia rīu rīkūrīirio, na kīmera.

English: Dry matter increases as the fruits mature.
Kikuyu: Dry matter yongererekaga o ūria itunda rīrakūra.

English: Fruits can be stored in a warm place (about 20 degrees temperatures) for a week.
Kikuyu: Makorobia no maigwo kūndū kūrī na ūrugarī mūnini (ithimo-inī coa 20oC) kwa ihinda ria kiumia kīmwe.

English: If the fruits ripen without shriveling, that is an indication that the fruits are mature.
Kikuyu: Makorobia mangīruha matekūhoha, monanagia atī nīmakūrū wega.

English: How are avocado fruits harvested?
Kikuyu: Hihi makorobia magethagwo atīa?

English: What are the types of layout designs that should be used in avocado planting?
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia ībatiī kūhandwo na njīra īrīkū?

English: There are different systems of layout, including the rectangular, hexagonal or square system.
Kikuyu: Nī kūrī mīthemba ngūrani ya mīhandīre, o hamwe na gūthondeka kīharo kīna kona inya itaiganaine, kona ithathatū kana ona kona inya ciganaine

English: In Kenya, the rectangular planting pattern is mainly used.
Kikuyu: Kaingī būrūri-inī wa Kenya, mīkorobia īhandagwo kīharo-inī gia kona inya itaiganaine.

English: This permits unhindered movement of machinery between the rows for cultivation, spraying, pruning and harvesting.
Kikuyu: ūndū ūyū nīwītīkagīria macini ihote kūgerera gatagatī ka raini cia mīkorobia ikīrīmaga, ikīhūūraga dawa, gūceha ona kūgetha.

English: It also allows maximum penetration and utilization of sunlight by the lower parts of the trees
Kikuyu: Ningī ūndū ūyū nīūteithagia riūa gūkinyīra honge cianathī na njīra njega.

English: What are the factors to consider when doing site selection for avocado planting?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū maroragwo hīndī ya gūcagūra mūgūnda mwega wa kūhanda mīkorobia?

English: This will be followed, if necessary, by levelling off to remove unwanted anthills.
Kikuyu: ūndū ūyū ūcoke ūrūmīrīrio, angīkorwo nī harī bata, kūiganania tīīrī na kweheria ithūa iria itangīendeka.

English: Land preparation is similar to that for other crops, namely one deep ploughing (30 - 40 cm) and two to three harrowings.
Kikuyu: Kūhaarīria mūgūnda kūhana o ta kwa mīmera īrīa ingī, na njīra ya gūcimba ūrikītie (ūriku wa 30 - 40 cm) na gūcokera maita merī nginya matatū.

English: To achieve a good tilth of the cleared land, fruit growers are advised to plant an annual crop a year before starting fruit cultivation.
Kikuyu: Nīguo gītira kia mūgūnda kiagīre, arīmī a matunda magīrīirwo kūhanda mīmera  ya kīmera kīmwe mwaka ūmwe mbere ya kūhanda matunda.

English: If frequent strong winds are expected, the early establishment of a windbreak hedge is necessary.
Kikuyu: Angīkorwo nī gūkoragwo na rūhuho rūnene kaingī, nī wega kūhanda rūgiri rwa kūhinga rūhuho.

English: Depending on location, tree species like Cupressus, Casuarina, Grevillea, etc. can be used for this purpose.
Kikuyu: Kūringana na matūra, mītī ta mūtarakwa, mūcindanūgū, mūbariti, na īngī no īhūthīrūo kūhinga rūhuho.

English: Unprotected avocado orchards will be affected by stunted growth, increased evaporation and erosion, defoliation, damaged branches, bruised or even blown off fruits.
Kikuyu: Mīgūnda ya mīkorobia īrīa ītarī na ūgitīīrī no ītūme mītī īhome, maaī maingī kūūra na riūa na tīīrī gūkuo nī kīguū, mītī gūita mathangū, honge kunīkanga, matunda kūgīa ironda kana kūgīa rangi wa gītīri.

English: If there is a choice, level or slightly sloping land is advantageous, otherwise contour cultivation or even terracing may be necessary
Kikuyu: Angīkorwo no ūthuure, mūgūnda mūigananu kana mūinamu hanini nīguo mwega, angīkorūo tiguo, no ūbatare gūthondeka benji.

English: What are the factors to consider when laying out a field for planting avocado?
Kikuyu: Hihi nī maūndū marīkū mūndū agīrīirwo nī gwīciria akībanga mūgūnda wa kūhanda mīkorobia?

English: When laying out the field, one should consider the type of soil and climatic conditions, tree type and size, irrigation, and farm machinery access.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa mūndū arabanga mūgūnda, nī agīrīirwo nī gwīciria ūhoro wa tīīrī wa mūgūnda ūcio na rīera, mūthemba wa mūtī na ūnene waguo, ūhei wa maaī, na ūhoro wa macini irīhūthīkaga.

English: What is the ideal spacing for hass and fuerte?
Kikuyu: Hihi mīkorobia mīthemba ya Hass na Fuerte ībatiī gūtaganio atīa hīndī ya kūhanda.

English: Fuerte and Hass are typically spaced at 9 x 9m (120 plants/ ha).
Kikuyu: Kaingī mītī ya Fuerte na Hass ītaganagio na mīta kenda gwa kenda (Mītī igana rīmwe rīa mīrongo īrī harī o hactare)

English: Prepare holes 60 x 60 x 60 cm (length x width x depth) a month before planting.
Kikuyu: Harīria marima mena ithimo cia 60 x 60 x 60 cm(ūraihu x wariī x ūriku) mweri ūmwe mbere ya kūhanda.

English: How many avocado trees can be planted in an īka in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Hihi īka īmwe ībatiī kūhandwo mītī īigana ya mīkorobia būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: The average avocado tree population per īka in Kenya is 150 trees
Kikuyu: īka īmwe būrūri-inī wa Kenya ībatiī kūhandwo mīkorobia igana rīmwe rīa mīrongo ītano.

English: What is the recommended spacing for planting avocados
Kikuyu: Hihi mītī ya mīkorobia ībatiī gūtaganio atīa hīndī ya kūhanda?

English:
Spacing of 7-10 m × 7-× 10m ( Hass- 7mx7m, Fuerte 9mx9m, Pinkerton 6mx6m) giving a population of 50-81
plants per īka.
Kikuyu: Ūtagananu wa mīta mūgwanja gwa ikūmi harī mītī ya Hass, Fuerte mita kenda gwa kenda, Pinkerton mita ithathatū gwa ithathatū irīa īkūrehe mūigana wa mītī mīrongo ītano nginya mīrongo īnana na īmwe harī o īka.

English: How should the holes for planting avocado be prepared before planting?
Kikuyu: Hihi marima ma kūhanda mīkorobia mabatiī kūharīrio atīa mbere ya kūhanda?

English: Separate the top and sub-soils.
Kikuyu: Gayūkania tīīri wa igūrū na wa nathī.

English: How is avocado planted?
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia īhandagwo atīa?

English: Make a basin around the seedling for holding water.
Kikuyu: Thondeka gītina-inī ūndū maaī marīrūgamaga hoo.

English: When is the ideal planting time for avocado?
Kikuyu: Nī hīndī īrīkū njega ya kūhanda mīkorobia?

English: Transplanting is more successful when carried out during the long rains either early morning or evening.
Kikuyu: Hindī njega ya gūthamia mīūngūrwa nī rīrīa kūrī na mbura nene ya kīmera mathaa na rūcinī tene kana hwainī.

English: In hot areas, shade the seedlings after transplanting.
Kikuyu: Nīwega gūthondekera mīūngūrwa ciīruru thutha wa kūmīthamia angīkorwo nīkūrī rīūa ihiū.

English: What are the steps involved in planting of hass avocado?
Kikuyu: Nī makinya marīkū magīrīire kūrūmīrīrwo hīndī ya kūhanda mīkorobia mīthemba ya Hass?

English: Prepare the land by removing stumps and plough the land up to 30cm
Kikuyu: Kūhaarīria mūgūnda na njīra ya kweheria ithukī na gūcimba mūgūnda ūcio na ūriku wa centimita mīrongo ītatū.

English: Ensure there are windbreakers such as hedges if the area has strong winds.
Kikuyu: Nīwega kūhanda ngiri cia kūhinga rūhuho angīkorwo itūra rīrī nīrīgūmagīrwo nī huho nene.

English: Dig holes 2ft by 2ft by 2ft.
Kikuyu: Enja marima ma futi igīrī kwa igīrī kwa igīrī.

English: Separate the top soil and the subsoil.
Kikuyu: Kūgayūkania tīri ūrīa ūrī igūrū na ūrīa ūrī na thīī.

English: Put the topsoil on the left side and the sub-soil on the right.
Kikuyu: Iga tīīri wa igūrū mwena wa ūmotho na wa nathīī mwena wa ūrīo.

English: Mix topsoil with two debes (buckets) of well-decomposed manure and a handful of NPK (20:10:10) fertilizer;
Kikuyu: Tukanaia tīīri wa igūrū na ndebe igīrī cia thumu mūhīu wega na wongerere rūhī rwa bataraitha ya NPK(20:10:10).

English: Fill back the soil-manure-fertilizer mix;
Kikuyu: Cokia mūtukanio ūyū wa tīīri-thumu-bataraitha irima-inī.

English: Remove the polythene, plant the Hass avocado seedling carefully and firm the soil around the hole;
Kikuyu: Ruta karatathi ka mūnyore, handa mūkorobia wa Hass ūmenyereire na ūkindīre tīīri irima-inī rīū.

English: It is now time to transplant your Hass avocado seedling.
Kikuyu: Rīu nī ihinda rīa gūthamia mīūngūrwa ya Hass.

English: Farmers are advised to transplant during the start of the rainy season.
Kikuyu: Arīmi magīrīirwo nī gūthamia mīūngūrwa īyo hīndī īrīa mbura īrambīrīria.

English: In Kenya is at the end of April.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīrī būrūri-inī wa Kenya nī mūthia-inī wa mweri wa īna.

English: This will enable your Hass avocados to establish themselves well on the farm.
Kikuyu: ūndū ūyū nī ūtūmaga mīūngūrwa ya Hass īhande wega

English: What is the ideal spacing for hass and how many trees can i plant per īka?
Kikuyu: Hihi mīkorobia ya Hass ībatiī gūtaganio atīa na nī mītī īgana ībatiī kūhandwo īka-inī īmwe?

English: The standard spacing for Hass avocado in Kenya is 5m by 5m.
Kikuyu: Kaingī mīkorobia ya Hass būrūri-inī wa kenya ītaganagio na mita ithano gwa ithano.

English: With this spacing, 150 Hass avocado trees can be planted in an īka.
Kikuyu: Gūtagania gūkū nīgūtūmaga mītī igana rīmwe ria mīrongo ītano īkahandīka īka-inī īmwe.

English: What are the factors to consider in nursery site selection?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū magīrīirūo gwīcirio hīndī ya gūcagūra handū ha gūthondeka natharī ya mīkorobia.

English: Nearness to quality water source;
Kikuyu: Handū hakūhīrīirīe kīhumo kia maaī.

English: Good drainage; Security (Animals, thieves etc.)
Kikuyu: tīīri ūraikūrūkia maaī wega; ūgitīri (Nyamū, aici, na maūndū mangī)

English: Accessibility to the road;
Kikuyu: Ūkinyanīria wa barabara;

English: Availability of wind breaks (Casuarina, Grevillea etc.)
Kikuyu: Handū harī na ngiri cia kūhinga rūhuho(mītarakwa, mībariti etc).

English: How should a nursery be established?
Kikuyu: Natharī ya mīkorobia ībatiī gūthondekwo atīa?

English: Prepare a seedbed of width 1 m and a desirable length by removing farm soil from the seedbed to a depth of 30 cm and refill the 30 cm depression with sand
Kikuyu: Harīria natharī īrī na wariī wa mita īmwe na ūraihu ūrīa wagīrīire na njīra ya kweheria tīīrī natharī-inī īyo nginya ūriku wa 30cm na ūcoke wīihūrie mūtaro ūcio na mūthanga.

English: What are the characteristics of mother trees to collect seed from?
Kikuyu: Hihi mīkorobia īrīa īhūthagīrūo kūruta mbeū īkoragwo na ngumo īrīkū?

English: Collect seed only from healthy and vigorous mother trees and from fruits which are mature and healthy rather than from off the ground
Kikuyu: Ūngania mbeū kuuma kūrī mītī ītarī mīrwaru na īrīa īrakūra na hinya na ni nīngī kuuma kūrī matunda marīa makūrū na mega matarī maragwa thīī.

English: What are  features of potting media?
Kikuyu: tīīri ūrīa wīkīragwo mīnyore-inī ya mīūngūrwa ūbatiī gūkorwo ūhana atīa?

English: Potting media: medium texture, sterilized if possible and with good drainage properties
Kikuyu: tīīrī ūyū ūbatiī gūkorwo ūrī mūhinyu hanini, ūtarī njamuthi na ūraikūrūkia maaī wega.

English: What are some of nursery management practices in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Njīra imwe cia kūmenyerera natharī ya mīkorobia nī irīkū?

English: Nurseries should be sited away from Pineapple fields as this is a source of Phytopthora cinnamoni infection.
Kikuyu: Natharī cia mīkorobia ibatiī gūthondekwo haraya na mūgūnda wa mananathi nīūndū nīmo kīhumo kia mūrimū mūrū wa Phytopthora Cinnamoni.

English: Avoid formation of hard pans on the potted plant by regularly pricking the soil with a sharp stick
Kikuyu: Girīrīria kūmagara gwa tīīrī ūrīa wīkīrītwo mīnyore-inī ya mīūngūrwa na njīra ya kūūhurahuria ūkīhūthīra gakerecū.

English: When are rootstocks ready for grafting?
Kikuyu: Nī rī gītina kia mūtī gīkoragwo kīrī gīkinyanīru gīa gūciarithania?

English: The rootstocks is ready for grafting 6 months after transplanting into biodegradable containers (6 X 9 Inches)
Kikuyu: Gītina kīa mūtī gīkinyanagīra gīgūciarithanio mīeri ītandatū thutha wa gūthamio kuuma tutainī na gwīkīrwo mīnyore-inī.

English: When are seedlings ready for transplanting after grafting?
Kikuyu: Nī hīndī īrīkū mīūngūrwa īkoragwo īrī mīkinyu ya kūhandwo thutha wa gūciarithanio?

English: 4 to 6 months later, the seedlings are ready for transplanting into the field and should be well healed and free from pests and diseases
Kikuyu: Thutha wa mīeri īna nginya ītandatū, mīūngūrwa mīciarithanie nīmīkinyanīru ya kūhandwo na ībatiī gūkorwo īrī mīhonu na īgītīirwo kuumana na tūtambi na mīrimū.

English: What is the most preferred time for transplanting of seedlings?
Kikuyu: Nī hīndī īrīkū njega makīria ya kūhanda mīūngūrwa ya mīkorobia?

English: Trees should be planted at the beginning of the seasonal rainfall and as soon as the rain has deeply penetrated the ground
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia yagīrīirwo kūhandwo kīambīrīria-inī kīa mbura ya kīmera na o rīrīa mbura yaringa na yaingīra tīīri-inī wega.

English: What are the factors to keep in mind when transplanting?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū magīrīirwo nī kūririkanwo hīndī ya kūhanda mīūngūrwa?

English: The potted plants are carefully removed from their eco-bags in such a way that the soil around the roots is not disturbed.
Kikuyu: Mīnyore ībatiī kūrutwo kahora tīīri ūrīa ūnyitīte mīri ūtegūthīnio.

English: Topsoil mixtures filled into the hole first, followed by the subsoil and firming down.
Kikuyu: Mūtukanio wa tīīri mūnoru wambīrīre gūthika irima, ūcoke ūrūmīrīrwo nī tīīri wa nathī na ūkindīrwo wega.

English: Never plant an Avocado tree deeper than the soil level it was in while in the eco-bag.
Kikuyu: Ndūkahande mūkorobia ūrikīire muno gūkīra ūriku wa harīa ūrakinyīte ūrī mūnyore-inī.

English: After transplanting, the leftover soil is used to build a basin around each tree in order to collect water from rain or irrigation
Kikuyu: Thutha wa kūhanda na kūihūria irima, tīīri ūrīa watigara ūhūthīrwo gūthondeka kīraī gīa kūhīngīrīria maaī hīndī ya mbura kana thutha wa kūhe mūtī maaī.

English: What are the major pest and disease control methods?
Kikuyu: Nīnjīra irīkū irīcia bata makīria cia kūgirīrīria tūtambi na mīrimū?

English: Integrated Crop Management (ICM) is the best option for food safety.
Kikuyu: Mūtukanio wa njīra itiganīte nīyo njīra īrīa njega makīria ya ūgitīīri wa tūtambi na mīrimū.

English: These methods include scouting of pests, field hygiene, proper spacing, physical methods like use of pheromone traps , biological methods and others that will only give option of using pesticides as last option.
Kikuyu: Njīra ici nīhamwe na gūcera mūgūnda-inī ūkīroraga tūtambi na mīrimū, kūiga ūtheru mūgūnda-inī, gūtagania mīkorobia wega hīndī ya kūhanda, ona kūhūthīra mītego ya kūgucīrīria tūtambi, njīra cia kīmerera, na njīra ingī iria iteithagīrīria ūhūthīri wa dawa ūkorwo ūrī ūndū wa mūthia.

English: How is False Codling Moth(FCM) controlled in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Hihi gatambi mūthemba wa False Codling Moth(FCM) kagiragīrīrio atīa mīkorobia-inī.

English: Proper orchard sanitation; Infested fruits (both on the tree and fallen) should be removed regularly (twice a week) and buried 50 cm or dipped in a drum filled with water mixed with used oil for 1 week.
Kikuyu: Ūtheru mwega wa mūgūnda; Matunda marwaru(marī mūtī-inī kana ona magwīte) mabatiī kweherio kaingī(maita merī o wiki) na mathikwo irima rīna ūriku wa 50cm kana maikio thīinī wa ibuba rīihūrītio maaī matukanīte ma oiro kwa ihinda ria wiki īmwe.

English: What are the symptoms of scab in avocado?
Kikuyu: Hihi ndariri cia ironda cia makorobia nīirīkū?

English: Lesions appear as small dark spots slightly raised, oval to elongated.
Kikuyu: Ironda ciambagīrīria kuoneka tarī maroro mairū manini na maimbu, marīna mūhianīre wa ovo ndaihūku.

English: These coalesce, giving a corky appearance to the surface of the fruits-impairing the appearance but not the internal quality of the fruit
Kikuyu: Maroro maya manenehaga, makanyitana na magathondeka kīronda kīnene ngothi-inī mategūthūkia na thīinī.

English: How do i control scab in avocados?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa kūgirīrīria mūrimū wa ironda cia ngothi ya makorobia?

English: Orchard sanitation:
Kikuyu: Ūtheru wa mūgūnda:

English: remove dead branches and twigs since they harbour the fungus.
Kikuyu: eheria honge na ngerecū iria nguū nīūndū ituīkaga mūciī wa tūtambi.

English: remove fallen rotten fruits from the field
Kikuyu: ūngania matunda marīa magwīte.

English: What are the symptoms of anthracnose in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia mūrimū wa anthracnose mīkorobia-inī nīirīkū?

English: Dry spots, dark brown in color, form on the skin, leading to abnormal development.
Kikuyu: Maroro maniaru na mairū methondekaga ngothi-inī ya makorobia na magakūra na mūhianīre ūtarīmwega.

English: In severe attacks, the young fruits drop.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūngīneneha, makorobia magūaga marī manini kuuma mūtī-inī.

English: It is mostly a post-harvest disease as the fruits are more susceptible when mature
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū kaingī, ūnyitaga makorobia marīa makūrū na magethetwo.

English: What are the symptoms of  Root Rot –Die Back in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia mūrimū wa mīrī kūbutha na honge kūūma mīthia-inī ūrīmi-inī wa makorobia nīirīkū?

English: Trees affected lose vitality, become sparsely foliated and do not produce a good crop.
Kikuyu: Mītī īrīa mīrwaru yagaga hinya wa gūciara, īkaruta mathangū manini na ndīrutaga maciaro maingī.

English: Leaves are pale-green and wilted, branches usually become sunburned and dieback in advanced stages.
Kikuyu: Mathangū magīaga rangi mūhunyūku wa ngirini na makahoha, honge ikahīa nī riūa na īgakua mīthia-inī mūrimū watherema.

English: Feeder roots get darkened and decay and trees will eventually die prematurely
Kikuyu: Mīri īrīa īheyaga mūtī irio īiraga na īkabutha na mūthia mūtī ūkooma ihinda rītarī ikinyu.

English: How do I control root rot die back?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa kūgirīrīria mūrimū wa mīri kūbutha na honge kūūma mīthia-inī?

English: Uprooting of affected trees.
Kikuyu: Kūmunya kwa mītī yoothe mīrwaru.

English: Hot water and fungicide treatment of seeds for propagation.
Kikuyu: Kūhūthīra maaīmahiū na dawa ya kūūraga njamuthi mūthemba wa fungi hīndīya kūharīria mbeū cia kūhanda.

English: Grafting on Phytophthora-tolerant and/or resistant rootstocks.Ridging or raised beds rootstocks
Kikuyu: Gūciarithania itina cia ndūire iria itahotagwo nī mīrimū ta Phytophthora.

English: What are the common pests that affect avocado production?
Kikuyu: Nītūtambi tūrīkū kaingī tūthīnagia mīkorobia?

English: Avocado thrips;Fruit flies;False codling moth;Scales;Red spider mites
Kikuyu: Tūtambi tūtū nī hamwe na thrips, kīhuruta, ngūci, mbūmbūī ndune

English: What are the common diseases that affect hass avocado in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī mīrimū īrīkū kaingī īnyitaga mīkorobia mūthemba wa Hass būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: Root rot and anthracnose
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa kūbūthia mīri na anthracnose

English: What are the symptoms of root rot in avocado production and how can it be controlled?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū monanagia atī mīri ya mīkorobia nīrabutha na mūrimū ūyū ūngīgirīrīrio atīa?

English: Root rot is a fungal disease that is caused by poorly drained soils and mostly occurs in areas prone to flooding.
Kikuyu: Kūbutha mīri nī mūrimū ūrehagwo nī jamuthi mūthemba wa fungi wonekanaga kūrīa tīīrī ūtaikūrūkagia maaī wega na kūndū kwarahaga maaī thutha wa mbura.

English: Trees have pale-green and wilted leaves and the branches are sunburned and die back.
Kikuyu: Mītī īkoragwo na mathangū ma rangi wa ngirini mūhunyūku na mahohu, na honge ikahīa nī riūa na īgakua mīthia-inī.

English: It is controlled by uprooting affected trees.
Kikuyu: Ūrigagīrīrio gūtherema na njīra ya kūmunya mītī yothe mīrwaru.

English: It can be prevented by keeping trees dry and treating seeds for propagation with hot water and fungicides.
Kikuyu: Ningī ūgiragīrīrio na njīra ya kūiga mītī īrī mīūmū na Kūhūthīra maaī mahiū na dawa ya kūūraga njamuthi mūthemba wa fungi hīndī ya kūharīria mbeū cia kūhanda.

English: What are symptoms of anthracnose in hass avocado and how can it be controlled?
Kikuyu: Ndariri cia mūrimū wa anthracnose mīkorobia-inī ya Hass nīirīkū na ūngīgirīrīrio atia?

English: Anthracnose is a fungal disease that attacks the fruit.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa anthracnose ūrīa ūrehagwo nī njamuthi mūthemba wa fungi ūnyitaga matunda.

English: The fruit develops dry, dark brown spots leading to abnormal development.
Kikuyu: Matunda magīaga maroro maniaru na mairū methondekete ngothi-inīya makorobia na magakūra na mūhianīre ūtarīmwega.

English: Mature fruits are most likely to be attacked but young fruits also drop.
Kikuyu: Kaingī matunda marīa makūrū nīmo marwaraga mūno o na gūkorwo marīa manini o namo nīmarwaraga na makagwa kuuma mītī-inī.

English: It is controlled by proper orchard sanitation and use of fungicides.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūyū ūrigagīrīrio na njīra ya kūiga mūgūnda ūrī mūtheru na dawa cia kūnina fungi

English: How does false codling moth manifest in avocado?
Kikuyu: Gatambi mūthemba wa kīhuruta kīyonanagia atīa mīkorobia-inī?

English: After emerging from the egg, the young caterpillar tunnels into the fruit and a discoloration appears at the point of entrance.
Kikuyu: Thutha wa kuuma itumbī-inī, kīgūnyū gīthegethaga ikorobia, naguo rangi wa harīa kīaingīrīra ūkagarūrūka.

English: While inside they feed on the pulp, causing premature ripening and fruit drop.
Kikuyu: Ihinda-inī rīu igunyū irī thīinī, irīaga ikorobia igatūma rīruhe tene rītarīikūrū na rīkagwa kuuma mūtī-inī.

English: How does thrips attack manifest itself in avocado?
Kikuyu: Tūtambi mūthemba wa thrips twīyonanagia atīa mīkorobia-inī?

English: These are small, slender insects with two pairs of fringed wings which feed by sucking the plant sap.
Kikuyu: Tūtū nī tūtambi tūnini, tūceke, tūrīna mathagu mana marīna guoya mīthiya-inī na tūnyuaga maaī ma mūmera.

English: They cause damage to the leaves and fruit and the affected parts become whitish or silvery and are usually covered by dark-coloured droppings.
Kikuyu: Twanangaga mathangū na matunda na kūrīa gūothe gūthūkangie gūkagīa na rangi mwerū kana wa betha na kaingī gūkahumbīrwo na kīoro kīrairīrīra.

English: Attacked fruits develop a leathery brown skin and feeding is most common on young fruits.
Kikuyu: Makorobia marīa mahutie magīaga makoni marīna rangi wa gītīīri, ūndū ūyū kaingī wonekaga makorobia-inī marīa manini.

English: How does scale infection manifest itself in avocado?
Kikuyu: Tūtambi mūthemba wa scales twīyonanagia atīa mīkorobia-inī?

English: These are small, stationary brown greenish insects commonly found sucking sap from avocado leaves.
Kikuyu: Tūtū nītūtambi tūnini, tūtaumaga harīa tūrī na twīrangi wa gītīīri na ngirini na kaingī tūnyuaga maaī mathangū-inī ma mīkorobia.

English: The soft scales excrete large amounts of honeydew, which lead to the development of sooty mold on leaves, branches and fruit.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi tūtū tuororo tūtigaga kioro kīingī kīhana ūūkī kīrīa gīcokaga gūcenjia gīkahana mūrarī mathangū-inī, honge-ini na matunda-inī

English: The honeydew also attracts ants. Armored scales may encrust young twigs, leaves and fruit and do not produce honeydew.
Kikuyu: Kīoro gīkī kīhana ūūkī nīkīgūcagīrīria mūthūa. Ngūci noikunīke honge iria nini, mahuti na matunda na hīndī īno itirutaga kīoro.

English: Damage can be serious on young trees and small twigs may be killed.
Kikuyu: Ūgwati no ūnenehe mītī-inī mīnini na honge iria nini no ciūme.

English: Although the presence of scales on the skin of fruit does not cause internal damage, it may lead to rejection of fruit, especially if grown for export.
Kikuyu: O na gūtuīka scales cikaire igūrū wa ikoro itithūkagia thīinī wa ikorobia, no gūtūme makorobia meregwo thoko-inī, na makīria thoko cia rūraya.

English: How is spider mites infection manifested in avocado production?
Kikuyu: Hihi ndariri cia mbūmbūī mīkorobia-inī nīirīkū?

English: Attack by spider mites produces circular necrotic spots covered by dense webbing.
Kikuyu: Tharīkīro ya mbūmbūī yonekaga thutha wa tūcigo tūthiūrūrī tūrabutha tūhumbīrītwo nī nenda cia mbūmbūī kwīyumīria

English: As mite populations increase, feeding causes leaf distortion and eventual drop.he growth of young trees is seriously affected and yields can be reduced significantly.
Kikuyu: O ūrīa mbūmbūī irongerereka ikīrīaga, mathangū nīmekūnjaga na thutha makagūa kuuma mūtī-inī. Mīkorobia īrīa mīnini ndīkūraga wega ona hanini na maciaro no manyihe biū.

English: At what temperature should avocado be pre-cooled?
Kikuyu: Hihi makorobia mabatiī kūigwo kūndū kwīna heho īigana atīa?

English: After harvesting, avocadoes must be cooled as quickly as possible to the optimum storage temperature of 5oC for Fuerte and Hass varieties; within 5 h of harvesting
Kikuyu: Mathaa-inī matano thutha wa kūgethwo, makorobia no mūhaka maigwo kūndū kūrīna heho o naihenya o ūrīa kūngīhoteka ithimo-inī cia 5°C harī mīthemba ya Fuerte na Hass.

English: How should hot water treatment be done?
Kikuyu: Maaī mahiū mahūgathīrwo atīa kūūraga njamuthi?

English: This treatment is used to kill fungal spores on and in the skin of the fruit.
Kikuyu: Maaī mahiū mahūthagīrwo kūūraga mbeū cia njamuthi iria irī igūrū na thīinī wa ikoro rīa ikoribia.

English: The avocados should be immersed in water heated to a temperature of 50°C for 3-5 min.
Kikuyu: Makorobia maikio thīinī wa maaī mahiūhītio kwa gīthimo kīa 50°C na marekwo maikare ndagīka ithatū nginya ithano.

English: Treatment of fruit using fungicides such as Bavistin (Carbendazim) and Sporta K Brochlura z improves the effectiveness of this treatment
Kikuyu: Kūhūthīra dawa cia kūnina tūtambi mūthemba wa fungi ta Bavistin (Carbendazim) na Sporta K Brochlura z nīkwagīrithagia ūrigitani ūyū makorobia-inī.

English: How is grading and packaging of avocado done?
Kikuyu: Makorobia mathuuranagio na kūbangwo irīti-inī atīa?

English: Avocado fruits are graded according to size and weight. Injured and diseased fruits should be removed.
Kikuyu: Makorobia mathuuranagio kūringana na ūrīa maigana na ūritū. Makorobia marīa marīna nguraro na marwaru mabatiī kweherio.

English: The fruits are packed as per the orders and quality standards in 4 kg cartons after all debris, soil and foreign particles are removed using a water bath.
Kikuyu: Makorobia mabangagwo kotoni-inī cia kiro inya kūringana na wendi wa mūgūri na ithimi icia njīra iria nginyanīru thutha wa kweheria indo ciothe itarendeka, tīīri ona indo ingī ūkīhūthīra maaī.

English: This water must be changed periodically to avoid cross contamination.
Kikuyu: Maaī maya mabatiī gūcenjio maita maigana ūna nīguo gūīthema gūtambia mīrīmū na gīko.

English: A fungicidal treatment (Thiabendazole) at 300 ml per 100 L of water is used.
Kikuyu: Maaī nīmongagīrīrwo dawa cia kunina fungi (Thiabendazole) na gīthimi kia 300ml harī rita 100 rīmwe ria maaī.

English: The fruits are waxed, and dried in hot air. Avocado fruits are stored in cold storage (5°C).
Kikuyu: Makorobia nīmahakagwo mūhūra na magacoka kūniario rīera-inī ihiū. Makorobia maigagwo kūndū kwīna heho ya githimo cia 5°C

English: How should avocado be packaged for transportation?
Kikuyu: Makorobia mohagwo atīa nīguo makuīke?

English: The fruits should be packaged in well ventilated cartons made of solid or corrugated fiberboard.
Kikuyu: Makorobia no mūhaka mabangwo katoni-inī njega ithondeketwo na mbodi cia faimba na iretīkīria rīera gūcera.

English: They should be arranged in a single layer.
Kikuyu: Katoni ici icoke ibangwo ciiganīrīirwo.

English: The boxes should have holes to allow for effective cooling and air circulation.
Kikuyu: Mathandūkū no mūhaka mekīrwo marima mīena ma kūhehia na gwītīkīria rīera gūcera

English: The boxes should be well labeled with the variety packed, size of fruits and a traceability number that’s easy to trace the produce from market to the farms in case of a quality concern in the market.
Kikuyu: Mathandūkū no mūhaka mekīrwo imenyithia ironania mūthemba wa makorobia ūrīa ūrīthīinī, thaithi ya matunda na namba ya kīmenyithanio īrīa īngīhotithia kūmenya mūgūnda ūrīa itunda riūmīte kuuma thoko-inī rīngīkorwo rīrīna thīna.

English: How should avocado for export be dispatched?
Kikuyu: Makorobia ma thoko cia rūraya magīrīirūo gūtwarwo na njīra īrīkū?

English: The packaged fruits should be palletized to allow for stacking of boxes to minimize on damage during transportation and movement of fruits within the boxes.
Kikuyu: Makorobia marīkia gwīkīrwo makatoni-inī, mabangwo mbareti-inī nīguo mabangīke na kūnyihia ngūraro hīndī ya ūkui kana rīrīa mararingana me thīinī wa makatoni.

English: The pallets should be hot water treated in a KEPHIS authorized facility
Kikuyu: Mbareti ibatiī gūthambio na maaī mahiū thīinīwa nyūmba īrīa yendekithītio nī KEPHIS

English: What are some of the  avocado varieties  grown in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nīmīthemba īrīkū ya mīkorobia īkūragio Kenya?

English: Kenya has over 40 varieties of avocado.
Kikuyu: Kenya gūkūragio mīthemba makīria ma mīrongo īna ya mīkorobia?

English: Hass is the main export variety and Fuerte is preferred for processing.
Kikuyu: Kaingī mūthemba wa Hass ūkūragio nīūndū wa thoko cia rūraya nayo Fuerte īkoragwo ya gūthondeka magūta.

English: Other commercial varieties are Keitt, Reed, Booth 8, Simmonds, Pinkerton, Nabal, Puebla, Tonnage, Ettinger, Hayes, G6 and G7.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba īngī īrīmagwo īrī ya biacara nīhamwe na Keitt, Reed, Booth 8, Simmonds, Pinkerton, Nabal, Puebla, Tonnage, Ettinger, Hayes, G6 na G7.

English: Varieties used as rootstocks include Puebla, Fuerte, Duke, G6, and G7.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba īrīa īhūthīkaga īrī ya gūciarithania mwena wa mīrī, kana nīyo īkīragwo mūrūri, nī Puebla, Fuerte, Duke, G6 na G7.

English: How do I identify hass avocado?
Kikuyu: Ingīhota atīa kūmenya ikorobia mūthemba wa Hass?

English: Bumpy, fairly thick skin that turns from green to purplish black when ripe;
Kikuyu: Ikoro rīkoragwo na mathundo na nīitungu na rīeruha, nīrīcenjagia rangī kuuma ngirini rīgatwīika ria papo mata.

English: It is round or oval in shape;
Kikuyu: Ningī ikorobia rīkoragwo rīhana mūbira kana ovo.

English: The flesh closest to the skin is a lime green color and as it nears the central pit it develops a rich creamy yellow tone;
Kikuyu: Rīonūrwo rīkoragwo na rangi mūnini wa ngirini ūrīa ūcenjagia ūgatuīka wa yellow mwena wa kūnyitana na mbegū.

English: It’s flesh has a buttery, creamy texture;
Kikuyu: Ikorobia rīrī rīkoragwo rīhana maguta.

English: It has a smooth, nutty and sweet taste
Kikuyu: rīgakorwo rīrī inyoroku, na mūrīo

English: It peels easily;
Kikuyu: Rīūnūkaga na ūhūthū

English: It has a large, round seed
Kikuyu: Rīkoragwo na mbegū nene ya gīthiūrūrī.

English: Hass avocados come in varying sizes but they typically weigh about 200g
Kikuyu: Makorobia mūthemba wa Hass makoragwo ma thaithi ngūrani no kaingī makoragwo na ūritū ta wa gramu magana merī.

English: Why is hass avocado the most preferred avocado fruit?
Kikuyu: Nīkīī gītūmaga ikorobia rīa Hass rīendeke mūno?

English: It is higher in fat than other varieties.
Kikuyu: Rīkoragwo na maguta maingī kūrī mīthemba īngī.

English: This fattiness gives it a smoother, creamier texture.
Kikuyu: Maguta maya matūmaga rīgakorwo rīrī inyoroku na rīkaha kīrimū.

English: Hass avocado trees have high yields compared to other varieties.
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia ya Hass īciaraga matunda maingī kūrī mīthemba īngī ya mīkorobia.

English: It has a longer shelf life;It has the most culinary uses;
Kikuyu: Ningī matūnda ma Hass maikaraga ihinda iraihu mategūthūka, na marīagwo na njīra nyingī itiganīte.

English: It is good for cooking because it is easy to mash and whip and richer in taste
Kikuyu: Matunda ma Hass nīmega kūruga nīūndū nīmakimīkaga na ūhūthū na gūkorogeka na nīmarī mūcamo mwega.

English: It withstands long distance shipping much easier than other varieties
Kikuyu: Nīmakuīkaga thabarī ndaya ya iria-inī gūkīra mīthemba īngī.

English: It has one of the longest harvesting seasons
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia ya Hass īgethagwo ihinda iraya

English: What are the benefits of Hass avocado in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nīūguni ūrīkū ūkoragwo na mīkorobia ya Hass būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: Hass is the most demanded avocado variety in Europe especially in The Netherlands and the United Kingdom (UK).
Kikuyu: Hass nī mūthemba wa makorobia marīa mendetwo mūno Europe makīrīa mabūrūri-inī ma Netherlands na United Kingdom (UK).

English: The Hass variety is very suitable for ripening;Hass avocado farmers earn two times more than farmers growing other avocado varieties.
Kikuyu: Mūthemba wa Hass wīruhaga wega; Arīmi a mīkorobia ya Hass mathūkūmaga maita merī makīria ma arīmi a mīkorobia mīthemba īngī.

English: The Kenya Plant Health Inspectorate Service (KEPHIS) says that avocado exports in 2019 were worth Sh10 billion of which 70% were Hass avocados.
Kikuyu: Rūhonge rwa Kenya Plant Health Inspectorate Service (KEPHIS) rwīrīgīte kuuga atī gīcunjī kia mīrongo mūgwanja kīa makorobia marīa mothe mendirio mabūrūri-inī ma nanja mwaka-inīwa 2019 marīa makinyirie shiringi birrioni ikūmi maarī ma mūthemba wa Hass.

English: Avocado exporters in Kenya buy Hass avocado at Sh10-Sh17 per fruit compared to Sh3 for Fuerte;
Kikuyu: Agūri arīa matwaraga makorobia mabūrūri-inīma rūraya magūraga o ikorobia rīa Hass kuuma shiringi ikūmi nginya ikūmi na mūgwanja narīo rīa Fuerte shiringi ithatū.

English: Hass avocado trees takes little space therefore small scale farmers can also venture into growing Hass avocados;
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia ya Hass ndīnenehaga mūno na kwogūo arīmi anini no mamīkūrīe.

English: Growing Hass avocado is easy and require less labor and time to maintain than the traditional cash crops like tea and coffee;
Kikuyu: Gūkūria mīkorobia ya Hass nīkūrī ūhūthū nīūndū gūtibataraga mīrūtīre mīingī ya wīra kana ona mathaa maingī ma kūmītungata ta mīmera īngī ya biacara ta macani na kahūa.

English: What are hass avocado tree?
Kikuyu: Hihi mūkorobia wa Hass ūhana atīa?

English: Hass trees are dense and evergreen;
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia ya Hass īkoragwo īrī mītumanu na ngirini hingo ciothe.

English: Have glossy, dark green leaves that have a vein-like pattern;
Kikuyu: īkoragwo īrīna mathangū mathaka, marīna rangi wa ngirini mūcamu

English: Their flowers are small and yellowish-green;
Kikuyu: Mahūa makoragwo marī manini na marangi wa ngoikoni na ngīrini.

English: They are fast-growing;
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia īno īkūraga naihenya.

English: They can grow up to 9m tall
Kikuyu: Noīraihe ngīnya mita kenda.

English: How long do hass avocado take to mature?
Kikuyu: Hihi mīkorobia ya Hass yoyaga ihinda rīigana atīa gūkūra?

English: Hass avocado trees begin bearing fruit after three to four years.
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia ya Hass yambagīrīria gūciara thutha wa mīaka ītatū nginya īna.

English: Hass avocado trees in Kenya flower in October and are ready for picking between June and September.
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia ya Hass būrūri-inī wa Kenya īcanūkaga mweri-inī wa ikūmi na matunda magakorwo marī makūrū gītagatī-inī kia mweri wa ītandatū nginya wa Kenda.

English: What are the indicators of hass avocado maturity?
Kikuyu: Makorobia makūrū ma hass mamenyekaga atīa?

English: Since Hass avocado is a dark variety it starts to change color from green to black or purple;
Kikuyu: Tondū makorobia ma Hass makoragwo mūthemba ūrīa mūtuku, manjagia gūcenjia rangi kuuma ngirini nginya papo njamu īrairīrīra.

English: Fruits that float on the surface when immersed in water are usually mature.
Kikuyu: Makorobia marīa makūrū mareraga igūrū me maaī-inī.

English: Immature fruits sink or float below the surface.
Kikuyu: Namo marīa matarī makūrū mareraga thīinī wa maaī kana makarika nathībīū.

English: Pick a few fruits and leave them to ripen in a warm place.
Kikuyu: Tua makorobia manini na ūmaige handū harīna ūrugarī meruhe.

English: If they soften evenly within a week at room temperature (about 20°C), then the trees are ready for picking
Kikuyu: Thutha wa kiumia kīmwe maigītwo handū hena ūrūgarī ta wa nyūmba(20oC), makorobia maya meruha wega monanagia atī nīmakinyu magūtuo.

English: What is the average yield per hass avocado tree?
Kikuyu: Hihi mūkorobia ūmwe wa Hass ūbatiī kūruta maciaro maigana atīa?

English: Hass avocado trees are very productive and can fruit all year round.
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia ya Hass nī īciaraga mūno na no īciare īrūmanīrīirīe mwaka wothe.

English: The first harvest comes after the second or third year.
Kikuyu: Maciaro ma mbere monekaga thutha wa mwaka wa kerī kana wa gatatū.

English: A farmer can harvest 70-100 fruits per tree.
Kikuyu: Mūrīmi no agethe makorobia mīrongo mūgwanja nginya igana rīmwe harī o mūtī.

English: After the fourth or fifth year, one tree can yield 400-800 fruits per season.
Kikuyu: Thutha wa mwaka wa kana kana wa wagatano, mūtī ūmwe no ūciare makorobia magana mana nginya magana manana.

English: That means a farmer can harvest between 60,000 and 120,000 fruits per īka.
Kikuyu: Ūguo nīkuga atī mūrīmi no agethe makorobia kuuma ngiri mīrongo ītandatū nginya ngiri igana rīmwe rīa mīrongo īrī harī īka.

English: After the six to seven years, a Hass avocado tree can yield 1000 fruits per year.
Kikuyu: Thutha wa mīaka ītandatū nginya mūgwanja, mūkorobia ūmwe wa Hass no ūciare matunda ngiri īmwe o mwaka.

English: Yield per tree increases gradually year after year as the tree gets older.
Kikuyu: Magetha harī o mūtī mongererekaga kahora o mwaka o ūrīa mūtī ūrakūra.

English: Proper wedding, pests and disease control and feeding of trees will yield more fruits per tree.
Kikuyu: Kūrīmīra na njīra njega, kūhiūrania na tūtambi na mīrimū na kūhe mītī irio nīgūtūmaga mītī īciare matunda maingī.

English: This will also improve the fruit size which in turn will yield better incomes
Kikuyu: Maūndū maya nīngī nīmatūmaga matunda manenehe ūndū ūrīa wongagīrīra ūmithio.

English: What are the main hass avocado export market destination
Kikuyu: Hihi thoko nene cia rūraya cia makorobia mūthemba wa Hass nīirīkū?

English: European Union;The Middle East;Russia;Singapore;Egypt;Hong Kong
Kikuyu: Ngwatanīro ya Europe, Gītarū kīa Arabu, Russia, Singapore, Egypt, Hong Kong.

English: What are the common features of fuerte avocado?
Kikuyu: Makorobia mūthemba wa Fuerte mahanaga atīa?

English: Fuerte is one of the major varieties in export markets.
Kikuyu: Fuerte nīīmwe ya mīthemba īrīa yendagio mūno mathoko-inīma mabūrūri ma rūraya.

English: The tree is broad, very productive and susceptible to both Anthracnose and Scab.
Kikuyu: Mūtī wa Fuerte ūkoragwo mūnene, ūciaraga mūno no nīūnyitagwo na ūhūthū nī mīrimū ya Anthracnose na Scab.

English: Pear-shaped fruit with medium seed.
Kikuyu: Matunda makoragwo na mūhianīre wa ngarīki na mbegū ītarīnene mūno?

English: Weight: 140 – 395 g per fruit.
Kikuyu: Ūritū: itunda rīa fuerte rīkoragwo na ūritū wa gramu 140-395.

English: Smooth thin green skin.
Kikuyu: Nīngī ikoro rīkoragwo rīrī inyoroku.

English: Skin remains green when fruits are mature.
Kikuyu: Ikoro rīticenjagia rangi wa ngirini ona itunda rīeruha.

English: Creamy, pale green flesh.
Kikuyu: Nyama yarīo īkoragwo na rangi wa kīrimū na ngirini theūku.

English: What are the common features of hass avocado?
Kikuyu: Makorobia mūthemba wa Hass mahanaga atīa?

English: The tree grows tall and requires cutting back;
Kikuyu: Mūtī nīūnenehaga na kwoguo nīūbataraga kūrengwo;

English: Distinctive for its skin that turns from green to purplish-black when fruits are mature;
Kikuyu: Matunda mamenyekaga na ngothi yamo īrīa īgarūrūkaga īgatuīka ndumanu īgatuīka ya papū īrairīrīra meruha;

English: Oval-shaped fruit with small to medium seed;
Kikuyu: Ikorobia rīkoragwo na mūhianīre wa oval na mbegū ītarīnene;

English: Easy peeling;
Kikuyu: Rīūnūkaga na ūhūthū;

English: Great taste;
Kikuyu: Mūcamo mwega mūno;

English: Outstanding shelf-life and shipping characteristics ;
Kikuyu: Rīoyaga ihinda rīnene gūthūka na nīrīega maūndū-inī megiī ūkui.

English: One of the longest harvesting seasons
Kikuyu: Nīmūthemba ūmwe ūgethagwo ihinda iraya.

English: What are the common features of pinkerton variety?
Kikuyu: Makorobia mūthemba wa Pinkerton mahanaga atīa?

English: Long, pear-shaped fruit with small seed ;
Kikuyu: Ikorobia rīkoragwo rīrī iraihu, mūhianīre wa oval na mbegū nini.

English: Large fruit that weighs 225 – 510 g per fruit;
Kikuyu: Itunda rīrīa inene nīrīkinyagia gramu 225 nginya 510

English: Medium thick green skin with slight pebbling;
Kikuyu: Ikoro tīitungu na nīrīrīthundo nini

English: Green skin deepens in color as it ripens;
Kikuyu: Rangi wa ngirini wa ikoro wūingīhaga o ūrīa rīreruha.

English: Excellent peeling;
Kikuyu: Rīūnūkaga na ūhūthū mūno.

English: What are the common features of the reed avocado variety?
Kikuyu: Makorobia mūthemba wa Reed mahanaga atīa?

English: Round fruit with medium seed.
Kikuyu: Matunda ma reed makoragwo marī magīthiūrūrī na mbegū nini.

English: Medium to large fruits with the weight of 225 – 510 g per fruit.
Kikuyu: Matunda manene nīmakinyagia gramu 225 nginya 510.

English: Thick green skin with slight pebbling.
Kikuyu: Ikoro itungu na nīrīrī thundo nini

English: Skin remains green when fruits are mature.
Kikuyu: Ikoro rīticenjagia rangī wa ngirini ona itunda rīeruha.

English: Creamy flesh with good taste.
Kikuyu: Rī rangi wa kīrimū na mūcamo mwega

English: Easy peeling
Kikuyu: Rīūnūkaga na ūhūthū

English: What are the common features of the publa avocado variety?
Kikuyu: Makorobia mūthemba wa publa ūngīmamenya atīa?

English: Small compact fruits resembling “Hass”.
Kikuyu: Makorobia manini mahana Hass

English: Skin of ripe fruit is black, thin and smooth.
Kikuyu: Ikoro rīa ikorobia rīīru nī rīirū, rīceke na inyoroku.

English: Fruit has a velvety flesh with nutty aroma and a rich butter taste
Kikuyu: Makorobia nīmanyoroku wega na mūtararīko mwega wa njūgū na mūcamo mwega ta wa iria.

English: What are the varieties used as rootstocks?
Kikuyu: Nīmīmera īrīkū īhūthagīrwo īrī gītina kīrīa gīciarithanagio?

English: Cultivars used as rootstocks are “Puebla” or “Local varieties”
Kikuyu: Mīmera īrīa īhūthagīrwo īrī gītina gīa gūciarithania nī Puebla kana mīhīrīga ya kīdūire.

English: Do avocado trees use a lot of water?
Kikuyu: Hihi mītī ya mīkorobia nī īhūthagīra maaī maingī?

English: Yes they do. A good avocado fruit consists of 73% water.
Kikuyu: Nīma nīīhūthagīra. Ikorobia rīega rīkoragwo rīrī na maaī gīcunjī kīa mīrongo mūganja na īthatū.

English: To achieve this, the crop must be sufficiently watered.
Kikuyu: Nīguo ūndū ūyū ūhinge, no mūhaka mīkorobia īheo maaī maiganu.

English: Water early in the morning because as the day progresses, the soil warm up.
Kikuyu: Mītī īheo maaī rūcinī tene tondū o ūrīa mūthenya ūrakīa, noguo tīīri ūrahiūha.

English: Watering in the evening can be done but is not recommended in places where temperatures drop very low because your soil will be too cold.
Kikuyu: Mīkorobia no īheo maaī hwainī ona gūtuīka gūtiagīrīire matūra-inī marīa kūhehaga mūno nīūndū tīīri nīūhehaga mūno.

English: This low moist soil temperature is a precursor for root rot disease manifested by small canopies, yellowing and a slow growing crop.
Kikuyu: Tīīrī mūhehu mūigū ūrehaga mūrimū wa kūbutha mīri ūrīa wīyonanagia na njīra ya mītī kwaga kūneneha, mathangū makagīa rangi wa yero na mīmera gūkūra kahora.

English: Avoid watering in the hot afternoons because water losses to evaporation will be high
Kikuyu: Wītheme gūitīīrīria mītī maaī rīrīa kūrīna riūa ihiū mūno tondū maaī maingī nīmagūcagio nī riūa.

English: How many litres of water does an avocado tree need?
Kikuyu: Mūtī wa mūkorobia ūbataraga maaī mūigana ūrīkū?

English: 70 litres daily.
Kikuyu: Rita mīrongo mūgwanja o mūthenya.

English: This is what must be present in the soil always to give you the recommended avocado yield per īka in Kenya of; 300-400 kgs (30,000 to 40,000 fruits) in the third to fourth year,
Kikuyu: Maūndū marīa no mūhaka makorwo tīīri-inī nīgetha mūkorobia ūkūhe magetha maingī thīinī wa eka īmwe nīta: 300-400kg, kana  matunda mūigana wa 30,000-40,000 mwaka-inī wa gatatū nginya wa īna

English: and 800 kgs-1 ton (80,000 to 100,000 fruits) from five years.
Kikuyu: Na kiro 800 nginya tani īmwe kana mūigana wa matunda 80,000 nginya 100,000 kuuma mwaka-inī wa ītano.

English: Avoid overwatering which causes leaves to curl, and the stems to soften weakening the plant structure
Kikuyu: Ndūkahe maaī makīria marīa matūmaga mathangū mekūnje na honge ihūthe na gūthūkia mūhianīre wa mūtī.

English: How much water does an avocado farm need?
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda wa mīkorobia ūbataraga maaī maigana atīa?

English: Your farm needs 25mm of water weekly (which is 101,200 litres) for a vibrant, well producing crop.
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda ūrabatara 25ml cia maaī harī wiki īmwe iria nī rita 101,200 kūrī mūtī ūrakūra na ūgaciara wega.

English: This translates to about 96 litres per plant daily assuming an average crop population of 150 trees per īka. 70 litres are enough, so the 26 extar litres (96-70) caters for losses due to evaporation
Kikuyu: ūū nītakuga rita 96 harī mūtī ūmwe o mūthenya angīkorwo mūigana nīwa mītī 150 ya mīkorobia harī eka īmwe. Rita 70 nīnjiganu nīguo icio ingī 26 ikanyuo nīriūa.

English: What is the best irrigation system for avocado trees?
Kikuyu: Nī njīra īrīkū njega ya gūitīīrīria mītī ya mīkorobia maaī?

English: It is recommended 2 key ways to irrigate your avocado crop. drip irrigation.
Kikuyu: Harī njīra igīrī cia bata ciendekithagio gūitīīrīria mītī ya mīkorobia maaī. Gūitīīrīria ūkīhūthīra mīberethi īna tūrima tūnini.

English: This is by the use of a button drip irrigation system.
Kikuyu: Njīra īno īhūthīkaga na mīberethi īna tūrima na mbatoni  cia kūhingūra.

English: The avocado drip irrigation cost in Kenya is from Kes 100,000 per īka.
Kikuyu: Njïra īno īhūthagīra ciringi ngiri igana rīmwe harī eka īmwe.

English: A button drip irrigation system is the best because it delivers water to the root zone where it is required, in drops.
Kikuyu: Gūitīīrīria maaī na njīra ya drip nīyo njega nīūndū īteithagīrīria gūtwara maaī haarīa mīri -inī harīa marabatarania.

English: This makes it water efficient because there is no wastage
Kikuyu: Njīra īno nīīteithagia ūhūthīri wa maaī na njīra njega nīūndū matiūraga.

English: How do I know when to water my avocado?
Kikuyu: Ingīmenya hīndī īrīa yagīrīire kūhe mītī ya mīkorobia maaī atīa?

English: Check your soil moisture to a depth of 10 cm.
Kikuyu: Rora ūgunyu wa tīīri ūriku-inī wa 10cm .

English: Test it by squeezing it in your hand.
Kikuyu: Thuthuria na kūhihinya tīīri na guoko.

English: If it holds together, then the moisture is enough.
Kikuyu: Angīkorwo nīukūnyitana, maaī marīa me tīīri-inī nīmaiganu.

English: If it crumbles, it is time to irrigate your avocado tree
Kikuyu: Ūngīrekania, nī hīndī njega ya kūhe tīīri maaī.

English: Do avocados need irrigation?
Kikuyu: Hihi mīkorobia nībataraga gūitīīrīrio maaī?

English: Yes they do, unless the crop is growing in an area that receives 0ver 40 inches or 1,016mm of rain annually.
Kikuyu: īīnīyagīrīire, tiga angīkorwo mūkorobia ūrakūra kūndū kuraga mbura makīria ma iji 40 kana milimita 1061 cia mbura o mwaka.

English: This is partly because the plant has shallow roots, hence requires water all year round
Kikuyu: Gītūmi nītondū mūkorobia ūkoragwo na mīri ītarikaga mūno, kuoguo īkabatara tīīri gūkorwo na maaī hīndī ciothe.

English: When is harvesting of avocado done in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Makorobia magethagwo hīndī īrīkū gūkū būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: Avocado is harvested between Mid-March and September in Kenya.
Kikuyu: Makorobia magethagwo gītagatī-inī kīa mweri wa ītatū nginya mweri wa Kenda gūkū būrūri-inī wa Kenya.
